headline: What BlackRock, State Street's rival QQQ ETFs mean for investors
summary: BlackRock (BLK) and State Street (SPY) are both launching exchange-traded funds (ETFs) to rival Invesco QQQ (QQQ), which tracks the NASDAQ-100 (^NDX).BNY global head of ETFs Ben Slavin joins Yahoo Finance to discuss what this news means for investors.

headline: BLK & STT Enter Nasdaq 100 Index ETFs, Challenge Invesco QQQ Fund
summary: BlackRock and State Street filings to launch Nasdaq 100 ETFs threaten Invesco's dominance, setting up a fee war in a $379B tech-focused market.

raw.headline: BLK & STT Enter Nasdaq 100 Index ETFs, Challenge Invesco QQQ Fund

headline: Shares of financial services companies are trading higher after President Trump's social media post indicated that the U.S. will work closely with Iran, and the countries have already agreed to several key points in the proposed conflict-resolution plan. The sector is also benefiting from easing energy prices and lower Treasury yields, which are helping to alleviate inflation concerns and support overall market sentiment.

headline: The 2026 Roth Catch-Up Rule Hits Workers Over $145,000: 4 ETFs to Make the Most of It
summary: Starting January 1, 2026, workers who earned more than $145,000 in FICA wages (Social Security wages, Box 3 of Form W-2) from their employer in the prior year can no longer make pre-tax catch-up contributions to their 401(k) or similar plan. Under Section 603 of the SECURE 2.0 Act, those contributions must now be designated ... The 2026 Roth Catch-Up Rule Hits Workers Over $145,000: 4 ETFs to Make the Most of It

headline: Is Invesco’s (IVZ) Tokenization Push Offsetting Rising Pressure On Its Core QQQ Franchise?
summary: In recent days, Invesco has moved to deepen its role in digital assets by becoming investment manager to Superstate’s roughly US$900 million tokenized Short Duration US Government Securities Fund, while also preparing a shareholder vote on bylaw changes to allow director removal with or without cause at its May 21, 2026 annual meeting, and now faces a prospective BlackRock iShares Nasdaq 100 ETF that would compete directly with its long-established QQQ franchise. Together, these developments...

headline: State Street Follows BlackRock With Filing to Challenge Invesco’s QQQ
summary: The company filed for a Nasdaq 100 ETF just one day after BlackRock’s iShares did the same.

raw.headline: State Street Follows BlackRock With Filing to Challenge Invesco’s QQQ
raw.summary: The company filed for a Nasdaq 100 ETF just one day after BlackRock’s iShares did the same.

headline: BlackRock, State Street Challenge Invesco's Nasdaq-100 ETF Dominance
summary: BlackRock and State Street have threatened Invesco's decade-long dominance on the Nasdaq-100 ETF space, filing with the SEC to launch competing funds tracking the same index. QQQ is currently the only ETF tracking the Nasdaq 100 ETF and is one of the most successful ETFs with $375.5 billion in AUM. With expense ratios yet to be disclosed, fees may emerge as the biggest win factor, potentially driving meaningful flows and reshaping competition in one of the ETF market's most lucrative segments.
